comment: One place to configure a run. Use it with: python pipeline.py countries --config run.config.json. Any value here can be overridden by passing the same flag on the command line. WHAT to collect lives in two companion files: categories.json (the countries/ccTLDs to search) and cc_links/exclusions.json (domains to skip). This file controls HOW the run behaves. Run `python pipeline.py countries --help` for every available key.

_how_to_edit.crawl: Which Common Crawl snapshot to use, e.g. CC-MAIN-2026-25.
_how_to_edit.categories_file: JSON mapping category name -> list of ccTLDs (edit categories.json to add/remove countries or whole regions).
_how_to_edit.per_category_limit: Max pages to collect per category (e.g. 100000).
_how_to_edit.max_per_domain: Cap pages taken from any single site so one big site doesn't dominate a country.
_how_to_edit.source: Where to fetch WARC records: 's3' (inside AWS/EC2, no throttle), 'cloudfront' (works anywhere, throttled), or use proxy below.
_how_to_edit.workers: Concurrent fetch threads. On 1 vCPU ~64 is plenty; scale with cores.
_how_to_edit.rate_limit: Max requests/sec to CloudFront (ignored for source=s3). ~35 safe direct, higher through proxies.
_how_to_edit.discover_delay: Seconds between index parts during discovery (paces direct parquet reads; 1-2s for large scans).
_how_to_edit.no_links: true = store only outlink counts (small DB); false = store the full link graph.
_how_to_edit.proxy: Single proxy / rotating-gateway URL, e.g. http://user:pass@host:port (leave null if unused).
_how_to_edit.proxy_file: File with one proxy per line (host:port:user:pass) for a rotating pool (leave null if unused).
